narrative: You spot a cluster of large broad leaves growing at the edge of the path. The lower stems have a distinctive reddish tinge...
interesting_fact: Broad-leaved Dock can produce around 60,000 seeds per year and they can remain viable in soil for up to 50 years — due to a chemical that inhibits microbial decay.

questions.question: Which of these plants is Dock — Broad-leaved / Curled?
Name the other common names for Broad-leaved Dock and Curled Dock
options: Bitter Dock and Yellow Dock
Goosegrass and Sticky Willy
Alehoof and Creeping Charlie
Winterweed and Starwort
questions.remember: Butter Dock is another name for Broad-leaved Dock — large leaves were traditionally used to wrap butter before waxed paper existed

questions.question: What are the botanical names of Broad-leaved and Curled Dock?
options: Rumex obtusifolius and Rumex crispus
Rumex acetosa and Rumex acetosella
Rumex alpinus and Rumex sanguineus
Rumex palustris and Rumex aquaticus

questions.question: Where is Dock typically found?
options: Field edges, roadsides, waste ground and woodland margins on fertile disturbed soil
Deep shaded woodland on heavy clay soil
Upland moorland and exposed hillsides on acidic peat
Coastal sand dunes and shingle beaches
questions.correct: Field edges, roadsides, waste ground and woodland margins on fertile disturbed soil

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Dock
options: Very large oval leaves with rounded tips
Lower leaves often have reddish stems
Tall reddish-brown seed heads visible well into winter
Strong minty smell when crushed
Tiny hooked hairs that cling to clothing
correct: Very large oval leaves with rounded tips
Lower leaves often have reddish stems
Tall reddish-brown seed heads visible well into winter

questions.question: How can you tell Broad-leaved Dock from Curled Dock?
options: Broad-leaved Dock has flat or slightly wavy leaf edges — Curled Dock has distinctly wavy and curled edges
Broad-leaved Dock has reddish stems — Curled Dock has entirely green stems
Broad-leaved Dock grows in woodland — Curled Dock only grows in open fields
Broad-leaved Dock flowers in spring — Curled Dock flowers in autumn
questions.correct: Broad-leaved Dock has flat or slightly wavy leaf edges — Curled Dock has distinctly wavy and curled edges
questions.remember: From a foraging perspective they are interchangeable — same uses, same taste, same risks

questions.question: What is Dock used for? Select all that apply
options: Young leaves cooked like spinach
Seeds ground into flour
Leaves used to wrap food parcels for cooking in embers
Root eaten as a vegetable
correct: Leaves used to wrap food parcels for cooking in embers
questions.remember: The dock leaf on nettle sting is largely folklore — a BMJ study found lettuce was just as effective. The cooling effect of any moist leaf provides some relief but there is no specific chemical in dock that treats nettle stings. Crushed nettles themselves are actually a better remedy

questions.question: What do Dock leaves taste like?
options: Bitter and slightly lemony — young spring leaves are milder
Sweet and fruity like pineapple
Sharp and strongly acidic like vinegar
Mild and vegetal like cooked spinach with no bitterness
questions.correct: Bitter and slightly lemony — young spring leaves are milder

questions.question: What are the cautions with Dock?
options: Contains oxalic acid — avoid in large quantities especially if prone to kidney stones
Contains calcium oxalate crystals causing immediate burning in the mouth
Avoid entirely during pregnancy as it stimulates uterine contractions
Safe in any quantity with no known cautions
questions.correct: Contains oxalic acid — avoid in large quantities especially if prone to kidney stones

questions.question: Which toxic plant could Dock be confused with?
options: Foxglove (Digitalis purpurea) — similar large leaves but matte and hairy with no rounded lobes at the base
Hemlock (Conium maculatum) — similar tall stems found in disturbed ground
Lords and Ladies (Arum maculatum) — similar large leaves in shaded habitats
Ragwort (Senecio jacobaea) — similar height and disturbed ground preference
questions.correct: Foxglove (Digitalis purpurea) — similar large leaves but matte and hairy with no rounded lobes at the base

questions.question: When does Dock flower?
options: Summer
Early spring
Late autumn
Midwinter

questions.question: What time of year is Dock most commonly found?
options: Year round — leaves best harvested in spring
Spring and summer only
Autumn and winter only
Summer only when fully grown
questions.correct: Year round — leaves best harvested in spring

questions.question: Is Dock native to the UK?
options: Yes — native throughout Britain and Ireland
No — introduced from Europe in the 17th century as a crop plant
Yes — but only in England and Wales, rare north of the border
Introduced by Roman settlers who used it as a medicinal herb
questions.correct: Yes — native throughout Britain and Ireland

questions.question: What was Dock historically used to treat? Select all that apply
options: Liver complaints and jaundice
Skin conditions and boils
Rheumatism and joint pain
Broken bones
questions.remember: Jaundice is the yellowing of the skin and whites of the eyes — caused by a build-up of bilirubin when the liver cannot process it properly. Usually a sign of liver problems

questions.question: How was Dock applied medicinally? Select all that apply
options: Fresh leaves crushed and applied as a poultice
Root made into a tea or tincture
Burned and inhaled as smoke
Ground into a powder and swallowed dry
correct: Fresh leaves crushed and applied as a poultice
Root made into a tea or tincture
questions.remember: Crushing leaves releases natural juices, tannins and in some cases anti-inflammatory compounds — the cooling effect of evaporating sap is also genuinely soothing. This is why crushed leaf poultices have been used across many cultures for thousands of years

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Dock?
options: Perennial — returns every year from a deep taproot
Annual — completes its full life cycle within a single growing season
Biennial — produces leaves in the first year and flowers in the second
Ephemeral — appears briefly in spring then dies back completely
questions.correct: Perennial — returns every year from a deep taproot

questions.question: What plant family does Dock belong to?
options: Polygonaceae — the dock and sorrel family
Rosaceae — the rose family
Asteraceae — the daisy family
Lamiaceae — the mint family
questions.correct: Polygonaceae — the dock and sorrel family

questions.question: What is the origin of the name Rumex?
options: From Latin rumo meaning to suck — Romans sucked sorrel leaves to quench thirst
From Latin rumex meaning sharp point — referring to the pointed seed heads
From Greek rhuma meaning flow — referring to its preference for wet habitats
From Latin ruber meaning red — referring to the reddish stems and seed heads
questions.correct: From Latin rumo meaning to suck — Romans sucked sorrel leaves to quench thirst

questions.question: What does Obtusifolius mean in the botanical name?
options: Blunt-leaved in Latin — referring to the rounded leaf tips
Broad-leaved in Latin — referring to the large size of the leaves
Bitter-leaved in Latin — referring to the bitter taste of the leaves
Oval-leaved in Latin — referring to the oval shape of the leaves
questions.correct: Blunt-leaved in Latin — referring to the rounded leaf tips

questions.question: Dock is listed under the UK Weeds Act 1959 — what does this mean?
options: It must be controlled on agricultural land to prevent it spreading to crops — it is not illegal to have it in a garden
It is illegal to grow, possess or forage it anywhere in the UK
It must be reported to the local council if found on private land
It can only be removed by licensed contractors using approved herbicides
questions.correct: It must be controlled on agricultural land to prevent it spreading to crops — it is not illegal to have it in a garden

questions.question: How long can Dock seeds remain viable in soil?
options: Up to 50 years
Up to 5 years
Up to 15 years
Up to 2 years
